summary: Descriptive overview of ipasproperty in substantive law: real vs personal property distinctions.
key_points: real vs personal property distinctions is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for ipasproperty are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine ipasproperty in litigation.
The doctrine of ipasproperty has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding ipasproperty requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
